steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: In right triangle XYZ, angle X has an opposite leg and the hypotenuse. By definition, $\sin X = \dfrac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}}$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Angles X and Y are complementary (sum to 90°). The side opposite X is the side adjacent to Y. So $\cos Y = \dfrac{\text{adjacent to }Y}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \dfrac{\text{opposite to }X}{\text{hypotenuse}}$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Therefore $\sin X = \cos Y$ exactly — they share the same numerator (the leg between the right angle and angle Y) and the same denominator (the hypotenuse).
